analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-362

POL-362.title: IMPLEMENT: Multi-language graph adapter rollout
POL-362.body:
## Objective

Expand Polaris graph coverage from TypeScript/JavaScript-only to all languages used in Polaris-managed repos (Evo and similar), by implementing language adapters for Svelte, Dart, Kotlin, Java, Swift, Python, Go, Rust, C, C++, C#, and shell scripts.

## Context

[POL-357](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-357/implement-multi-language-graph-adapter-architecture) / PR #118 delivered the adapter chassis: `LanguageAdapter` interface, `AdapterRegistry`, `GraphCapabilityRegistry`, extraction pipeline with file-level fallback, and the TypeScript/JavaScript adapter. Only TS/JS is covered today.

[POL-361](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-361/analyze-remaining-graph-language-adapters-and-implementation-rollout) analyzed the rollout requirements and produced this implementation plan. Without language adapters for the remaining ecosystems, Polaris can only see unsupported files at file level and cannot provide reliable function/symbol-level impact analysis across the full repo.

Analysis artifact: `docs/Polaris/analysis/pol-361-graph-language-adapter-rollout.md`

## Goal

Implement 6 clusters of language adapters in priority order, resulting in symbol-level graph coverage for all Evo-critical and common languages, while keeping the file-level fallback intact and safe.

## Scope

* src/graph/adapter/svelte/\*\*
* src/graph/adapter/dart/\*\*
* src/graph/adapter/kotlin-java/\*\*
* src/graph/adapter/swift/\*\*
* src/graph/adapter/python/\*\*
* src/graph/adapter/go/\*\*
* src/graph/adapter/rust/\*\*
* src/graph/adapter/c/\*\*
* src/graph/adapter/cpp/\*\*
* src/graph/adapter/csharp/\*\*
* src/graph/adapter/shell/\*\*
* src/graph/adapter/registry.ts
* src/graph/adapter/**fixtures**/mixed-repo/\*\*

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 6 child clusters implemented and passing
- [ ] `GraphCapabilityReport` includes symbol-level coverage for every new adapter language
- [ ] No silent file disappearance for any supported or unsupported extension
- [ ] Mixed-language fixture repo builds cleanly via `npm run polaris -- graph build`
- [ ] All new adapters declare `confidence` and `limitations` per the LanguageAdapter interface

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npm run polaris -- graph build (against mixed-language fixture)

## Ordering

Execute children in order: C-01 → C-02 → C-03 → C-04 → C-05 → C-06. Each is independent but priority reflects Evo criticality. All children can be dispatched in parallel if capacity allows.

## Non-goals

* Do not modify the LanguageAdapter interface or graph schema unless a child issue explicitly requires it.
* Do not add SmartDocs/Markdown route-graphing in this cluster.
* Do not implement config dependency edges (DEPENDS_ON edge type) — follow-on cluster.
* Do not claim full Evo readiness until all 6 clusters are complete and validated.

POL-361.title: Analyze remaining graph language adapters and implementation rollout

POL-368.title: IMPLEMENT: C, C++, C#, and shell graph adapters
POL-368.body:
## Objective

Implement four language adapters — C, C++, C#, and shell scripts — completing the full language adapter rollout. These cover Windows/platform code (C, C++, C# for Flutter Windows runner) and shell operational scripts.

## Context

[POL-357](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-357/implement-multi-language-graph-adapter-architecture) established the adapter chassis. [POL-361](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-361/analyze-remaining-graph-language-adapters-and-implementation-rollout) identified this as Cluster 6 — lower urgency than Evo-critical languages but needed for complete platform coverage. `tree-sitter-c@0.24.1`, `tree-sitter-cpp@0.23.4`, and `tree-sitter-c-sharp@0.23.5` are all high-quality. Shell uses regex-based extraction (no tree-sitter grammar needed for phase 1).

Analysis artifact: `docs/Polaris/analysis/pol-361-graph-language-adapter-rollout.md`

## Goal

Four `LanguageAdapter` implementations:

* **C** (`.c`, `.h`): functions, structs as `class`-kind, `#include` as `import`-kind
* **C++** (`.cpp`, `.cc`, `.cxx`, `.hpp`): classes, functions, methods, `#include` as `import`-kind
* **C#** (`.cs`): classes, static functions, methods, `using` directives as `import`-kind
* **Shell** (`.sh`, `.bash`, `.zsh`): regex-based function extraction, `confidence: "low"`

## Scope

* src/graph/adapter/c/adapter.ts
* src/graph/adapter/c/extract.ts
* src/graph/adapter/c/runtime.ts
* src/graph/adapter/c/index.ts
* src/graph/adapter/c/fixtures/
* src/graph/adapter/cpp/adapter.ts
* src/graph/adapter/cpp/extract.ts
* src/graph/adapter/cpp/runtime.ts
* src/graph/adapter/cpp/index.ts
* src/graph/adapter/cpp/fixtures/
* src/graph/adapter/csharp/adapter.ts
* src/graph/adapter/csharp/extract.ts
* src/graph/adapter/csharp/runtime.ts
* src/graph/adapter/csharp/index.ts
* src/graph/adapter/csharp/fixtures/
* src/graph/adapter/shell/adapter.ts
* src/graph/adapter/shell/extract.ts
* src/graph/adapter/shell/index.ts
* src/graph/adapter/shell/fixtures/
* src/graph/adapter/registry.ts
* src/graph/adapter/**fixtures**/mixed-repo/sample.c
* src/graph/adapter/**fixtures**/mixed-repo/sample.cpp
* src/graph/adapter/**fixtures**/mixed-repo/sample.cs
* src/graph/adapter/**fixtures**/mixed-repo/sample.sh
* package.json (add tree-sitter-c, tree-sitter-cpp, tree-sitter-c-sharp)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `CAdapter`: `languageId: "c"`, `fileExtensions: [".c", ".h"]`, confidence `"high"`
- [ ] `CppAdapter`: `languageId: "cpp"`, `fileExtensions: [".cpp", ".cc", ".cxx", ".hpp"]`, confidence `"high"`
- [ ] `CSharpAdapter`: `languageId: "csharp"`, `fileExtensions: [".cs"]`, confidence `"high"`
- [ ] `ShellAdapter`: `languageId: "shell"`, `fileExtensions: [".sh", ".bash", ".zsh"]`, confidence `"low"`, regex-based
- [ ] C: extracts function declarations, struct declarations (as `class`-kind), `#include` (as `import`-kind)
- [ ] C++: extracts class declarations, function definitions, method declarations, `#include` (as `import`-kind)
- [ ] C#: extracts class declarations, method declarations, static functions, `using` directives (as `import`-kind)
- [ ] Shell: extracts function definitions via regex (`function foo()` and `foo()` patterns)
- [ ] All four registered in `getDefaultAdapterRegistry()`
- [ ] Unit tests per adapter covering core symbol kinds
- [ ] `npm run build` passes
- [ ] `npm test` passes

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/graph/adapter/c src/graph/adapter/cpp src/graph/adapter/csharp src/graph/adapter/shell

## Ordering

Sixth and final cluster. No dependency on other clusters. Lower priority (3) than Evo-critical clusters. Can be dispatched after C-01 through C-04 are complete, or in parallel if capacity allows.

## Non-goals

* Do not use tree-sitter for shell scripts — regex is sufficient for phase 1.
* Do not add C/C++ preprocessor macro expansion.
* Do not implement C# generic type resolution.
* Do not modify the LanguageAdapter interface.

POL-367.title: IMPLEMENT: Python, Go, and Rust graph adapters
POL-367.body:
## Objective

Implement three language adapters — Python, Go, and Rust — using their respective high-quality `tree-sitter` grammars. These are lower-risk adapters with excellent grammar coverage and straightforward symbol extraction patterns.

## Context

[POL-357](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-357/implement-multi-language-graph-adapter-architecture) established the adapter chassis. [POL-361](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-361/analyze-remaining-graph-language-adapters-and-implementation-rollout) identified Python/Go/Rust as Cluster 5 (common tooling repos). All three have excellent grammar packages: `tree-sitter-python@0.25.0`, `tree-sitter-go@0.25.0`, `tree-sitter-rust@0.24.0`.

Analysis artifact: `docs/Polaris/analysis/pol-361-graph-language-adapter-rollout.md`

## Goal

Three `LanguageAdapter` implementations:

* **Python** (`.py`, `.pyi`): classes, functions, methods, import/from-import
* **Go** (`.go`): structs/interfaces as `class`-kind, top-level functions/methods, import blocks
* **Rust** (`.rs`): structs/enums/traits as `class`-kind, functions, impl-block methods, `use` declarations

All three declare `confidence: "high"` and document limitations.

## Scope

* src/graph/adapter/python/adapter.ts
* src/graph/adapter/python/extract.ts
* src/graph/adapter/python/runtime.ts
* src/graph/adapter/python/index.ts
* src/graph/adapter/python/fixtures/
* src/graph/adapter/go/adapter.ts
* src/graph/adapter/go/extract.ts
* src/graph/adapter/go/runtime.ts
* src/graph/adapter/go/index.ts
* src/graph/adapter/go/fixtures/
* src/graph/adapter/rust/adapter.ts
* src/graph/adapter/rust/extract.ts
* src/graph/adapter/rust/runtime.ts
* src/graph/adapter/rust/index.ts
* src/graph/adapter/rust/fixtures/
* src/graph/adapter/registry.ts
* src/graph/adapter/**fixtures**/mixed-repo/sample.py
* src/graph/adapter/**fixtures**/mixed-repo/sample.go
* src/graph/adapter/**fixtures**/mixed-repo/sample.rs
* package.json (add tree-sitter-python, tree-sitter-go, tree-sitter-rust)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `PythonAdapter`: `languageId: "python"`, `fileExtensions: [".py", ".pyi"]`, confidence `"high"`
- [ ] `GoAdapter`: `languageId: "go"`, `fileExtensions: [".go"]`, confidence `"high"`
- [ ] `RustAdapter`: `languageId: "rust"`, `fileExtensions: [".rs"]`, confidence `"high"`
- [ ] Python: extracts class, def (top-level as `function`, class-level as `method`), import/from-import as `import`-kind
- [ ] Go: extracts struct/interface (as `class`-kind), func (as `function` or `method` based on receiver), import block entries
- [ ] Rust: extracts struct/enum/trait (as `class`-kind), fn (top-level as `function`, impl-block as `method`), use declarations
- [ ] All three registered in `getDefaultAdapterRegistry()`
- [ ] Unit tests per adapter: class, function, method, import; edge cases per language
- [ ] `npm run build` passes
- [ ] `npm test` passes

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/graph/adapter/python src/graph/adapter/go src/graph/adapter/rust

## Ordering

Fifth cluster. No dependency on C-01 through C-04. Can run in parallel with other clusters.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement call/reference extraction for any of these languages in phase 1.
* Do not add Python type stub deep analysis beyond `.pyi` file handling.
* Do not model Go generics or Rust lifetimes as distinct symbol kinds.
* Do not modify the LanguageAdapter interface.

POL-366.title: IMPLEMENT: Swift graph adapter
POL-366.body:
## Objective

Implement a Swift language adapter for the Polaris graph using `tree-sitter-swift` to extract classes, structs, protocols, functions, methods, and import declarations from `.swift` files.

## Context

[POL-357](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-357/implement-multi-language-graph-adapter-architecture) established the adapter chassis. [POL-361](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-361/analyze-remaining-graph-language-adapters-and-implementation-rollout) identified Swift as the fourth-priority adapter for Evo iOS/macOS targets. `tree-sitter-swift@0.7.1` is available but older; the adapter targets declaration-level symbols only, which are stable in the grammar.

Analysis artifact: `docs/Polaris/analysis/pol-361-graph-language-adapter-rollout.md`

## Goal

A `SwiftAdapter` implementing `LanguageAdapter` that:

* Handles `.swift` files
* Uses `tree-sitter-swift` for symbol extraction
* Extracts classes, structs, protocols (all as `class`-kind), top-level functions, methods, and import declarations
* Declares `confidence: "medium"` and documents grammar-version limitations

## Scope

* src/graph/adapter/swift/adapter.ts
* src/graph/adapter/swift/extract.ts
* src/graph/adapter/swift/runtime.ts
* src/graph/adapter/swift/index.ts
* src/graph/adapter/swift/fixtures/
* src/graph/adapter/registry.ts
* src/graph/adapter/**fixtures**/mixed-repo/sample.swift
* package.json (add tree-sitter-swift dependency)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SwiftAdapter` implements `LanguageAdapter` with `languageId: "swift"` and `fileExtensions: [".swift"]`
- [ ] `tree-sitter-swift` added to package.json dependencies
- [ ] Extracts `class`-kind for class, struct, and protocol declarations
- [ ] Extracts `function`-kind for top-level function declarations
- [ ] Extracts `method`-kind for class/struct/extension method declarations
- [ ] Extracts `import`-kind for `import` declarations
- [ ] `confidence: "medium"`, `limitations` document grammar age risk and deferred async/await/macro coverage
- [ ] `SwiftAdapter` registered in `getDefaultAdapterRegistry()`
- [ ] Unit tests cover: class, struct, protocol, function, method, extension methods, import
- [ ] `npm run build` passes
- [ ] `npm test` passes

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/graph/adapter/swift

## Ordering

Fourth cluster. No dependency on C-01, C-02, or C-03. Can run in parallel with other clusters.

## Non-goals

* Do not extract Swift async/await or macro symbols in phase 1.
* Do not model Swift/Objective-C bridge code beyond import symbols.
* Do not modify the LanguageAdapter interface.
* Do not upgrade the grammar — file a follow-on issue if tree-sitter-swift improves.

POL-365.title: IMPLEMENT: Kotlin and Java graph adapters
POL-365.body:
## Objective

Implement Kotlin and Java language adapters for the Polaris graph using `tree-sitter-kotlin` and `tree-sitter-java`, sharing common extraction helpers where feasible.

## Context

[POL-357](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-357/implement-multi-language-graph-adapter-architecture) established the adapter chassis. [POL-361](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-361/analyze-remaining-graph-language-adapters-and-implementation-rollout) identified Kotlin/Java as the third-priority adapter cluster for Evo Android targets. `tree-sitter-java@0.23.5` is high quality; `tree-sitter-kotlin@0.3.8` is older but covers class/method/import at declaration level.

Analysis artifact: `docs/Polaris/analysis/pol-361-graph-language-adapter-rollout.md`

## Goal

Two `LanguageAdapter` implementations — `JavaAdapter` and `KotlinAdapter` — sharing extraction logic via common helpers:

* Java: `.java` files, classes/interfaces/methods/import statements
* Kotlin: `.kt`, `.kts` files, classes/objects/functions/methods/import directives
* Both declare appropriate `confidence` and `limitations`

## Scope

* src/graph/adapter/kotlin-java/java-adapter.ts
* src/graph/adapter/kotlin-java/kotlin-adapter.ts
* src/graph/adapter/kotlin-java/extract-common.ts
* src/graph/adapter/kotlin-java/java-extract.ts
* src/graph/adapter/kotlin-java/kotlin-extract.ts
* src/graph/adapter/kotlin-java/runtime.ts
* src/graph/adapter/kotlin-java/index.ts
* src/graph/adapter/kotlin-java/fixtures/
* src/graph/adapter/registry.ts
* src/graph/adapter/**fixtures**/mixed-repo/sample.java
* src/graph/adapter/**fixtures**/mixed-repo/sample.kt
* package.json (add tree-sitter-java, tree-sitter-kotlin)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `JavaAdapter` with `languageId: "java"`, `fileExtensions: [".java"]`
- [ ] `KotlinAdapter` with `languageId: "kotlin"`, `fileExtensions: [".kt", ".kts"]`
- [ ] Java: extracts class, interface (as `class`-kind), method, import
- [ ] Kotlin: extracts class, object (as `class`-kind), fun (top-level as `function`, class-level as `method`), import
- [ ] Both registered in `getDefaultAdapterRegistry()`
- [ ] `JavaAdapter` confidence: `"high"`; `KotlinAdapter` confidence: `"medium"` (grammar age)
- [ ] `KotlinAdapter` limitations document grammar version risk
- [ ] Unit tests: Java class, interface, methods, imports; Kotlin class, object, fun, imports
- [ ] `npm run build` passes
- [ ] `npm test` passes

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/graph/adapter/kotlin-java

## Ordering

Third cluster. No dependency on C-01 or C-02. Can run in parallel with other clusters.

## Non-goals

* Do not model Android resource XML files as symbol-level nodes.
* Do not implement Kotlin/Java cross-language call edges in phase 1.
* Do not modify the LanguageAdapter interface.

POL-364.title: IMPLEMENT: Dart graph adapter
POL-364.body:
## Objective

Implement a Dart language adapter for the Polaris graph using `tree-sitter-dart` to extract classes, functions, methods, and import directives from `.dart` files.

## Context

[POL-357](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-357/implement-multi-language-graph-adapter-architecture) established the adapter chassis. [POL-361](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-361/analyze-remaining-graph-language-adapters-and-implementation-rollout) identified Dart/Flutter as the second-highest priority adapter for Evo. `tree-sitter-dart@1.0.0` is available on npm and provides stable grammar coverage for class/function/import extraction.

Analysis artifact: `docs/Polaris/analysis/pol-361-graph-language-adapter-rollout.md`

## Goal

A `DartAdapter` implementing `LanguageAdapter` that:

* Handles `.dart` files
* Uses `tree-sitter-dart` for symbol extraction
* Extracts: classes, top-level functions, class methods, import directives
* Represents Flutter widgets as `class`-kind symbols
* Declares `confidence: "medium"` and documents limitations

## Scope

* src/graph/adapter/dart/adapter.ts
* src/graph/adapter/dart/extract.ts
* src/graph/adapter/dart/runtime.ts
* src/graph/adapter/dart/index.ts
* src/graph/adapter/dart/fixtures/
* src/graph/adapter/registry.ts
* src/graph/adapter/**fixtures**/mixed-repo/sample.dart
* package.json (add tree-sitter-dart dependency)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `DartAdapter` implements `LanguageAdapter` with `languageId: "dart"` and `fileExtensions: [".dart"]`
- [ ] `tree-sitter-dart` added to package.json dependencies
- [ ] Extracts `class`-kind for class declarations (including Widget subclasses)
- [ ] Extracts `function`-kind for top-level function declarations
- [ ] Extracts `method`-kind for class method declarations
- [ ] Extracts `import`-kind for `import '...'` and `import 'package:...'` directives
- [ ] `resolveImportSpecifier` handles relative and package imports
- [ ] `confidence: "medium"`, `limitations` documented (call extraction deferred)
- [ ] `DartAdapter` registered in `getDefaultAdapterRegistry()`
- [ ] Unit tests cover: class, function, method, import, Flutter widget class
- [ ] `npm run build` passes
- [ ] `npm test` passes

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/graph/adapter/dart

## Ordering

Second cluster. No dependency on C-01. Can run in parallel with C-01.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement call/reference extraction in phase 1.
* Do not model Flutter platform channel patterns beyond class/method symbols.
* Do not modify the LanguageAdapter interface or graph schema.

POL-363.title: IMPLEMENT: Svelte graph adapter
POL-363.body:
## Objective

Implement a Svelte language adapter for the Polaris graph that extracts symbols from `.svelte` files by delegating `<script>` block content to the existing TypeScript/JavaScript adapter.

## Context

[POL-357](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-357/implement-multi-language-graph-adapter-architecture) established the adapter chassis. [POL-361](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-361/analyze-remaining-graph-language-adapters-and-implementation-rollout) analyzed the rollout plan. Svelte is the highest-priority adapter because it is Evo's primary frontend language. Analysis determined that `tree-sitter-svelte@0.11.0` is too old to be reliable; the delegation strategy reuses the high-confidence TS/JS adapter instead.

Analysis artifact: `docs/Polaris/analysis/pol-361-graph-language-adapter-rollout.md`

## Goal

A `SvelteAdapter` implementing `LanguageAdapter` that:

* Handles `.svelte` files
* Extracts `<script>` and `<script lang="ts">` block contents via text parsing
* Delegates symbol extraction to the existing `TypeScriptJavaScriptAdapter`
* Emits the component name (derived from filename) as a `class`-kind symbol
* Declares `confidence: "medium"` and documents limitations

## Scope

* src/graph/adapter/svelte/adapter.ts
* src/graph/adapter/svelte/extract.ts
* src/graph/adapter/svelte/index.ts
* src/graph/adapter/svelte/fixtures/
* src/graph/adapter/registry.ts
* src/graph/adapter/**fixtures**/mixed-repo/sample.svelte

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SvelteAdapter` implements `LanguageAdapter` interface with `languageId: "svelte"` and `fileExtensions: [".svelte"]`
- [ ] `<script>` and `<script lang="ts">` blocks are extracted and delegated to the TS/JS adapter
- [ ] Component name (from filename, no extension) is emitted as an exported `class`-kind symbol
- [ ] Files with no `<script>` block produce only the component-name symbol with no errors
- [ ] `confidence: "medium"`, `limitations` declared
- [ ] `SvelteAdapter` registered in `getDefaultAdapterRegistry()` in registry.ts
- [ ] Unit tests cover: basic component, `<script lang="ts">`, no-script file, multiple exports
- [ ] `npm run build` passes
- [ ] `npm test` passes

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/graph/adapter/svelte

## Ordering

First cluster in the rollout. No dependencies on other adapter clusters. Can start immediately after [POL-362](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-362/implement-multi-language-graph-adapter-rollout) is created.

## Non-goals

* Do not use `tree-sitter-svelte` — use delegation strategy only.
* Do not extract markup-level symbols (reactive declarations, template refs) in phase 1.
* Do not add `<style>` block parsing.
* Do not modify the LanguageAdapter interface.
